message:
fix(pager): cold-reset all cache tiers on fast-path mode transitions to prevent stale page resurrection

The page cache's fast-path mode (single-connection optimization) previously
left pages behind when switching between fast-path and sharded modes. Pages
inserted during fast-path mode would linger in the fast array after switching
to sharded mode, and vice versa. A subsequent mode switch back could then
surface these stale pages, silently overwriting newer data written in the
other mode.

enable_fast_path():
- When transitioning from sharded to fast-path mode, clear ALL tiers: the
  fast array, flat slots, every shard, and eviction history — ensuring a
  completely cold restart with no hidden residual pages from the now-inactive
  sharded cache
- Idempotent when already in fast-path mode (no-op)

disable_fast_path():
- When transitioning from fast-path to sharded mode, call clear() which
  now covers all tiers — the fast array is cleared even though it's
  becoming inactive, preventing stale pages from reappearing if fast-path
  is later re-enabled
- Idempotent when already in sharded mode (pages preserved)

clear():
- Always clear the fast array (if allocated) regardless of current mode
  flag — previously only cleared the fast array when use_fast_path was
  true, leaving hidden pages in the inactive fast array that could
  resurface after a mode switch

Test coverage (5 new tests):
- test_disable_fast_path_is_idempotent_for_sharded_cache: calling disable
  when already in sharded mode preserves existing sharded pages
- test_enable_fast_path_clears_now_inactive_sharded_tiers: switching to
  fast-path drops all sharded pages; switching back doesn't resurrect them
- test_enable_fast_path_drops_stale_fast_array_pages: fast-path pages from
  a prior session don't reappear after a sharded write and re-enable
- test_sharded_cache_clear_removes_inactive_fast_path_pages: clear() wipes
  hidden fast-array pages even when currently in sharded mode
- Expanded existing fast_path_basic test with disable-clears and re-enable
  cold-reset assertions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
